page.content: The following data table presents a detailed analysis of the Osteoporosis Self-Assessment Tool for the elderly (OSTA) index, categorized by various thresholds of T-scores, specifically T ≤ -2.5 and T ≤ -2.0. These thresholds are critical for assessing the risk of osteoporosis-related complications among different patient groups, including those undergoing outpatient (OP) and non-outpatient (non-OP) treatments.

page.content: The data highlights the percentage of individuals experiencing adverse outcomes, with particular focus on the metrics of hospitalization and mortality. For instance, the "Deathn (%)" column elucidates the proportion of patients who succumbed to complications related to osteoporosis, while the "Hospitalizationn (%)" column indicates the frequency of hospital admissions due to osteoporosis-related issues. The overall percentages presented under the "Totaln (%)" column provide a comprehensive overview of the impact of osteoporosis across the studied population. This analysis serves as a crucial tool for healthcare professionals in identifying at-risk patients and implementing targeted preventive measures to mitigate the associated health risks.
